StringBuffer.java

00036 obligated to do so.  If you do not wish to do so, delete this
00037 exception statement from your version. */
00038 
00039 package java.lang;
00040 
00041 import java.lang.System;
00042 
00074 public final class StringBuffer
00075 {
00081     int count;
00082 
00087     char[] value;
00088 
00095     boolean shared;
00096 
00100     private static final int DEFAULT_CAPACITY = 16;
00101 
00105     public StringBuffer()
00106     {
00107         this(DEFAULT_CAPACITY);
00108     }
00109 
00117     public StringBuffer(int capacity)
00118     {
00119         value = new char[capacity];
00120     }
00121 
00130     public StringBuffer(String str)
00131     {
00132         // Unfortunately, because the size is 16 larger, we cannot share.
00133         count = str.count;
00134         value = new char[count + DEFAULT_CAPACITY];
00135         str.getChars(0, count, value, 0);
00136     }
00137 
00147     public synchronized int length()
00148     {
00149         return count;
00150     }
00151 
00160     public synchronized int capacity()
00161     {
00162         return value.length;
00163     }
00164 
00175     public synchronized void ensureCapacity(int minimumCapacity)
00176     {
00177         ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(minimumCapacity);
00178     }
00179 
00192     public synchronized void setLength(int newLength)
00193     {
00194         if (newLength < 0)
00195           throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00196 
00197         int valueLength = value.length;
00198 
00199         /* Always call ensureCapacity_unsynchronized in order to preserve
00200          * copy-on-write semantics.  */
00201         ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(newLength);
00202 
00203         if (newLength < valueLength)
00204         {
00205             /* If the StringBuffer's value just grew, then we know that
00206              * value is newly allocated and the region between count and
00207              * newLength is filled with '\0'.  */
00208             count = newLength;
00209         }
00210         else
00211         {
00212             /* The StringBuffer's value doesn't need to grow.  However,
00213              * we should clear out any cruft that may exist.  */
00214             while (count < newLength)
00215                 value[count++] = '\0';
00216         }
00217     }
00218 
00226     public synchronized char charAt(int index)
00227     {
00228         if (index < 0 || index >= count)
00229             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00230 
00231         return value[index];
00232     }
00233 
00249     public synchronized void getChars(int srcOffset, int srcEnd,
00250         char[] dst, int dstOffset)
00251     {
00252         if (srcOffset < 0 || srcEnd > count || srcEnd < srcOffset)
00253             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00254 
00255         System.arraycopy(value, srcOffset, dst, dstOffset, srcEnd - srcOffset);
00256     }
00257 
00266     public synchronized void setCharAt(int index, char ch)
00267     {
00268         if (index < 0 || index >= count)
00269             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00270 
00271         // Call ensureCapacity to enforce copy-on-write.
00272         ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(count);
00273         value[index] = ch;
00274     }
00275 
00286     public StringBuffer append(Object obj)
00287     {
00288         return append(obj == null ? "null" : obj.toString());
00289     }
00290 
00298     public synchronized StringBuffer append(String str)
00299     {
00300         if (str == null)
00301             str = "null";
00302 
00303         int len = str.count;
00304         ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(count + len);
00305         str.getChars(0, len, value, count);
00306         count += len;
00307         return this;
00308     }
00309 
00320     public synchronized StringBuffer append(StringBuffer stringBuffer)
00321     {
00322         if (stringBuffer == null)
00323             return append("null");
00324 
00325         synchronized (stringBuffer)
00326         {
00327             int len = stringBuffer.count;
00328             ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(count + len);
00329             System.arraycopy(stringBuffer.value, 0, value, count, len);
00330             count += len;
00331         }
00332 
00333         return this;
00334     }
00335 
00346     public StringBuffer append(char[] data)
00347     {
00348         return append(data, 0, data.length);
00349     }
00350 
00365     public synchronized StringBuffer append(char[] data, int offset, int count)
00366     {
00367         if (offset < 0 || count < 0 || offset > data.length - count)
00368             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00369 
00370         ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(this.count + count);
00371         System.arraycopy(data, offset, value, this.count, count);
00372         this.count += count;
00373         return this;
00374     }
00375 
00385     public StringBuffer append(boolean bool)
00386     {
00387         return append(bool ? "true" : "false");
00388     }
00389 
00396     public synchronized StringBuffer append(char ch)
00397     {
00398         ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(count + 1);
00399         value[count++] = ch;
00400         return this;
00401     }
00402 
00412     // This is native in libgcj, for efficiency.
00413     public StringBuffer append(int inum)
00414     {
00415         return append(String.valueOf(inum));
00416     }
00417 
00427     public StringBuffer append(long lnum)
00428     {
00429         return append(Long.toString(lnum, 10));
00430     }
00431 
00441     public StringBuffer append(float fnum)
00442     {
00443         return append(Float.toString(fnum));
00444     }
00445 
00455     public StringBuffer append(double dnum)
00456     {
00457         return append(Double.toString(dnum));
00458     }
00459 
00471     public synchronized StringBuffer delete(int start, int end)
00472     {
00473         if (start < 0 || start > count || start > end)
00474           throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00475 
00476         if (end > count)
00477             end = count;
00478 
00479         // This will unshare if required.
00480         ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(count);
00481 
00482         if (count - end != 0)
00483             System.arraycopy(value, end, value, start, count - end);
00484 
00485         count -= end - start;
00486         return this;
00487     }
00488 
00497     public StringBuffer deleteCharAt(int index)
00498     {
00499         return delete(index, index + 1);
00500     }
00501 
00513     public StringBuffer insert(int offset, Object obj)
00514     {
00515         return insert(offset, obj == null ? "null" : obj.toString());
00516     }
00517 
00528     public synchronized StringBuffer insert(int offset, String str)
00529     {
00530         if (offset < 0 || offset > count)
00531             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00532 
00533         if (str == null)
00534             str = "null";
00535 
00536         int len = str.count;
00537         ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(count + len);
00538         System.arraycopy(value, offset, value, offset + len, count - offset);
00539         str.getChars(0, len, value, offset);
00540         count += len;
00541         return this;
00542     }
00543 
00554     public StringBuffer insert(int offset, char[] data)
00555     {
00556         int i;
00557 
00558         for (i = offset; i < data.length; i++)
00559             value[i] = data[i - offset];
00560 
00561         return this;
00562     }
00563 
00575     public StringBuffer insert(int offset, boolean bool)
00576     {
00577         return insert(offset, bool ? "true" : "false");
00578     }
00579 
00588     public synchronized StringBuffer insert(int offset, char ch)
00589     {
00590         if (offset < 0 || offset > count)
00591             throw new StringIndexOutOfBoundsException();
00592 
00593         ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(count + 1);
00594         System.arraycopy(value, offset, value, offset + 1, count - offset);
00595         value[offset] = ch;
00596         count++;
00597         return this;
00598     }
00599 
00611     public StringBuffer insert(int offset, int inum)
00612     {
00613         return insert(offset, String.valueOf(inum));
00614     }
00615 
00627     public StringBuffer insert(int offset, long lnum)
00628     {
00629         return insert(offset, Long.toString(lnum, 10));
00630     }
00631 
00643     public StringBuffer insert(int offset, float fnum)
00644     {
00645         return insert(offset, Float.toString(fnum));
00646     }
00647 
00659     public StringBuffer insert(int offset, double dnum)
00660     {
00661         return insert(offset, Double.toString(dnum));
00662     }
00663 
00670     public synchronized StringBuffer reverse()
00671     {
00672         // Call ensureCapacity to enforce copy-on-write.
00673         ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(count);
00674 
00675         for (int i = count >> 1, j = count - i; --i >= 0; ++j)
00676         {
00677             char c = value[i];
00678             value[i] = value[j];
00679             value[j] = c;
00680         }
00681 
00682         return this;
00683     }
00684 
00693     public String toString()
00694     {
00695         // The string will set this.shared = true.
00696         return new String(this);
00697     }
00698 
00708     private void ensureCapacity_unsynchronized(int minimumCapacity)
00709     {
00710         if (shared || minimumCapacity > value.length)
00711         {
00712             // We don't want to make a larger vector when `shared' is
00713             // set.  If we do, then setLength becomes very inefficient
00714             // when repeatedly reusing a StringBuffer in a loop.
00715             int max = (minimumCapacity > value.length
00716                 ? value.length * 2 + 2 : value.length);
00717 
00718             minimumCapacity = (minimumCapacity < max ? max : minimumCapacity);
00719             char[] nb = new char[minimumCapacity];
00720             System.arraycopy(value, 0, nb, 0, count);
00721             value = nb;
00722             shared = false;
00723         }
00724     }
00725 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:24 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
